2: Caveats and Considerations: It is important to note that this is an exemplary
3: illustration and not production-ready code. For real-world applications, several factors
4: must be taken into account. An LLM may not fully grasp the intended meaning of a
5: goal and might incorrectly assess its performance as successful. Even if the goal is
6: well understood, the model may hallucinate. When the same LLM is responsible for
7: both writing the code and judging its quality, it may have a harder time discovering it
8: is going in the wrong direction.
9: Ultimately, LLMs do not produce flawless code by magic; you still need to run and test
10: the produced code. Furthermore, the "monitoring" in the simple example is basic and
11: creates a potential risk of the process running forever.
